title: Pseudobaeckea squalida
taxonomy: Pseudobaeckea squalida is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudobaeckea, which is a part of the order Ericales. The Ericaceae family is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as heath or heather family. Within the Ericaceae family, Pseudobaeckea squalida is a unique species native to Australia. Its classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Pseudobaeckea squalida, also known as the "Kalahari sage", is a small shrub native to the arid regions of southern Africa, characterized by its unique, small, white flowers and silvery foliage. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health issues, such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pseudobaeckea squalida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe and heal the skin.
